quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'heart'?
explanation: Cardi/o derives from Greek 'kardia' (heart). Found in cardiology, cardiologist, tachycardia, and electrocardiogram.
statement: The combining form for heart is cardi/o.
wowFactor: Every time you hear 'cardio' at the gym, you are using the ancient Greek word for heart!

variants.question: Cardi/o is the combining form for which organ?
A cardiologist specializes in the ___.

visualDescription: English HEART transforming into Greek CARDI/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'liver'?
explanation: Hepat/o derives from Greek 'hepar' (liver). Found in hepatitis, hepatology, hepatomegaly, and hepatocyte.
statement: The combining form for liver is hepat/o.
wowFactor: Hepatitis literally means 'inflammation of the liver' — hepar (liver) + itis (inflammation).

variants.question: Hepat/o is the combining form for which organ?
Hepatitis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English LIVER transforming into Greek HEPAT/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'kidney'?
explanation: Nephr/o derives from Greek 'nephros' (kidney). Found in nephrology, nephritis, nephrectomy, and nephron.
statement: The combining form for kidney is nephr/o.
wowFactor: The nephron is the basic filtering unit of the kidney — every kidney has about one million of them!

variants.question: Nephr/o is the combining form for which organ?
A nephrologist specializes in diseases of the ___.

visualDescription: English KIDNEY transforming into Greek NEPHR/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'brain'?
explanation: Encephal/o derives from Greek 'enkephalos' (brain). Found in encephalitis, encephalopathy, electroencephalogram (EEG), and encephalocele.
statement: The combining form for brain is encephal/o.
wowFactor: An EEG (electroencephalogram) literally means 'electric brain writing' — it records the brain's electrical activity.

variants.question: Encephal/o is the combining form for which organ?
Encephalitis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English BRAIN transforming into Greek ENCEPHAL/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'lung'?
explanation: Pulmon/o derives from Latin 'pulmo' (lung). Found in pulmonology, pulmonary, pulmonologist, and cardiopulmonary.
statement: The combining form for lung is pulmon/o.
wowFactor: Pulmonary embolism (a clot in the lung) gets its name directly from this Latin root for lung.

variants.question: Pulmon/o is the combining form for which organ?
Pulmonary arteries carry blood to the ___.

visualDescription: English LUNG transforming into Latin PULMON/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'stomach'?
explanation: Gastr/o derives from Greek 'gaster' (stomach). Found in gastrology, gastritis, gastrointestinal, gastroscopy, and gastroenterology.
statement: The combining form for stomach is gastr/o.
wowFactor: A gastropod (snail or slug) literally means 'stomach-foot' in Greek — they crawl on their muscular belly!

variants.question: Gastr/o is the combining form for which organ?
A gastroenterologist treats diseases of the ___ and intestines.

visualDescription: English STOMACH transforming into Greek GASTR/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'bone'?
explanation: Oste/o derives from Greek 'osteon' (bone). Found in osteoporosis, osteology, osteoblast, osteoclast, and osteomyelitis.
statement: The combining form for bone is oste/o.
wowFactor: Osteoporosis literally means 'porous bones' — oste/o (bone) + por/o (pore) + osis (condition).

variants.question: Oste/o is the combining form for which tissue?
An osteopath focuses on conditions of the ___ and musculoskeletal system.

visualDescription: English BONE transforming into Greek OSTE/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'joint'?
explanation: Arthr/o derives from Greek 'arthron' (joint). Found in arthritis, arthroscopy, arthropathy, arthroplasty, and arthrodesis.
statement: The combining form for joint is arthr/o.
wowFactor: Arthropods (insects, spiders, crustaceans) are named for their 'jointed legs' — the same Greek root as arthritis!

variants.question: Arthr/o is the combining form for which structure?
Arthritis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English JOINT transforming into Greek ARTHR/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'muscle'?
explanation: My/o derives from Greek 'mys' (muscle). Found in myology, myopathy, myocardium, myositis, and electromyography (EMG).
statement: The combining form for muscle is my/o.
wowFactor: The myocardium is literally the 'muscle of the heart' — the cardiac muscle that pumps blood with every beat.

variants.question: My/o is the combining form for which tissue?
Myopathy is a disease of the ___ tissue.
Which term uses the combining form my/o?

visualDescription: English MUSCLE transforming into Greek MY/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'nerve'?
explanation: Neur/o derives from Greek 'neuron' (nerve, sinew). Found in neurology, neuritis, neuron, neuralgia, neurosurgery, and neuropathy.
statement: The combining form for nerve is neur/o.
wowFactor: The word 'neuron' itself comes directly from this Greek root — every neuron in your brain is named with this combining form!

variants.question: Neur/o is the combining form for which structure?
A neurologist specializes in diseases of the ___ system.

visualDescription: English NERVE transforming into Greek NEUR/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'skin'?
explanation: Derm/o (also dermat/o) derives from Greek 'derma' (skin). Found in dermatology, dermis, dermatitis, hypodermic, and epidermis.
statement: The combining form for skin is derm/o.
wowFactor: The word 'epidermis' literally means 'upon skin' — epi (upon) + derm (skin) + is (layer).

variants.question: Derm/o is the combining form for which tissue?
A dermatologist specializes in diseases of the ___.

visualDescription: English SKIN transforming into Greek DERM/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'eye'?
explanation: Ophthalm/o derives from Greek 'ophthalmos' (eye). Found in ophthalmology, ophthalmoscope, ophthalmologist, and ophthalmoplegia.
statement: The combining form for eye is ophthalm/o.
wowFactor: Ophthalmology is one of the hardest medical specialties to spell — but knowing ophthalm/o (eye) + logy (study of) makes it logical!

variants.question: Ophthalm/o is the combining form for which organ?
An ophthalmologist specializes in diseases of the ___.

visualDescription: English EYE transforming into Greek OPHTHALM/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'ear'?
explanation: Ot/o derives from Greek 'ous/otos' (ear). Found in otology, otitis, otoscope, otolaryngology (ENT), and otorrhea.
statement: The combining form for ear is ot/o.
wowFactor: An ENT doctor (Ear, Nose, Throat) is formally an otolaryngologist — ot/o (ear) + laryng/o (larynx) + logist (specialist).

variants.question: Ot/o is the combining form for which organ?
Otitis media is inflammation of the middle ___.

visualDescription: English EAR transforming into Greek OT/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'nose'?
explanation: Rhin/o derives from Greek 'rhis/rhinos' (nose). Found in rhinology, rhinitis, rhinoplasty, rhinoscopy, and rhinovirus.
statement: The combining form for nose is rhin/o.
wowFactor: The rhinoceros takes its name from this root — rhino (nose) + ceros (horn) means 'nose-horned animal'!

variants.question: Rhin/o is the combining form for which organ?
Rhinoplasty is a surgical procedure on which structure?

visualDescription: English NOSE transforming into Greek RHIN/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'mouth'?
explanation: Stomat/o derives from Greek 'stoma' (mouth, opening). Found in stomatology, stomatitis, stoma (surgical opening), and xerostomia (dry mouth).
statement: The combining form for mouth is stomat/o.
wowFactor: When surgeons create an ostomy (like a colostomy), the 'stoma' is the artificial mouth-like opening on the body surface.

variants.question: Stomat/o is the combining form for which structure?
Stomatitis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English MOUTH transforming into Greek STOMAT/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'tongue'?
explanation: Gloss/o derives from Greek 'glossa' (tongue). Found in glossology, glossitis, glossodynia, hypoglossal nerve, and glossopharyngeal.
statement: The combining form for tongue is gloss/o.
wowFactor: The hypoglossal nerve (cranial nerve XII) literally means 'under the tongue' — it controls almost all tongue movements.

variants.question: Gloss/o is the combining form for which organ?
Glossitis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English TONGUE transforming into Greek GLOSS/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'tooth'?
explanation: Dent/o derives from Latin 'dens/dentis' (tooth). Found in dentistry, dentist, dental, denture, dentin, and orthodontics.
statement: The combining form for tooth is dent/o.
wowFactor: Orthodontics comes from Greek: ortho (straight) + odont (tooth) + ics — it literally means 'straightening teeth'!

variants.question: Dent/o is the combining form for which structure?
A dentist specializes in health of the ___.

visualDescription: English TOOTH transforming into Latin DENT/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'pharynx'?
explanation: Pharyng/o derives from Greek 'pharynx' (throat). Found in pharyngology, pharyngitis, pharyngoscopy, and nasopharynx.
statement: The combining form for pharynx is pharyng/o.
wowFactor: The pharynx is the crossroads of breathing and swallowing — food goes forward to the esophagus, air goes back to the larynx.

variants.question: Pharyng/o is the combining form for which structure?
Pharyngitis (a sore throat) is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English PHARYNX transforming into Greek PHARYNG/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'larynx'?
explanation: Laryng/o derives from Greek 'larynx' (voice box). Found in laryngology, laryngitis, laryngoscopy, laryngectomy, and laryngospasm.
statement: The combining form for larynx is laryng/o.
wowFactor: Laryngitis makes your voice hoarse or disappear entirely because the larynx contains your vocal cords.

variants.question: Laryng/o is the combining form for which structure?
Laryngitis is inflammation of the ___, causing hoarseness.

visualDescription: English LARYNX transforming into Greek LARYNG/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'trachea'?
explanation: Trache/o derives from Greek 'tracheia arteria' (rough artery). Found in tracheotomy, tracheostomy, tracheitis, and tracheobronchitis.
statement: The combining form for trachea is trache/o.
wowFactor: A tracheotomy cuts into the trachea (windpipe) to create an emergency airway — a life-saving procedure done with a simple incision.

variants.question: Trache/o is the combining form for which structure?
A tracheotomy creates a surgical opening in the ___.

visualDescription: English TRACHEA transforming into Greek TRACHE/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'bronchus'?
explanation: Bronch/o derives from Greek 'bronchos' (windpipe). Found in bronchoscopy, bronchitis, bronchodilator, bronchospasm, and bronchopneumonia.
statement: The combining form for bronchus is bronch/o.
wowFactor: Bronchodilators (inhalers) literally 'widen the bronchi' — dilat (widen) + or (thing that does) applied to the bronch/o (airways).

variants.question: Bronch/o is the combining form for which structure?
Bronchitis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English BRONCHUS transforming into Greek BRONCH/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'esophagus'?
explanation: Esophag/o derives from Greek 'oisophagos' (gullet/food carrier). Found in esophagoscopy, esophagitis, esophagectomy, and gastroesophageal reflux.
statement: The combining form for esophagus is esophag/o.
wowFactor: GERD (gastroesophageal reflux disease) combines gastr/o (stomach) + esophag/o (esophagus) — describing exactly where the acid travels.

variants.question: Esophag/o is the combining form for which structure?
Esophagitis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English ESOPHAGUS transforming into Greek ESOPHAG/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'intestine'?
explanation: Enter/o derives from Greek 'enteron' (intestine). Found in enterology, enteritis, gastroenterology, gastroenteritis, and parenteral nutrition.
statement: The combining form for intestine is enter/o.
wowFactor: Gastroenteritis (stomach flu) literally means 'inflammation of the stomach and intestines' — exactly what causes those miserable symptoms!

variants.question: Enter/o is the combining form for which structure?
Enteritis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English INTESTINE transforming into Greek ENTER/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'colon'?
explanation: Col/o (also colon/o) derives from Greek 'kolon' (large intestine). Found in colonoscopy, colitis, colostomy, colectomy, and colorectal.
statement: The combining form for colon is col/o.
wowFactor: A colonoscopy uses col/o (colon) + scopy (visual examination) — a camera explores the entire large intestine to screen for cancer.

variants.question: Col/o is the combining form for which structure?
Colitis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English COLON transforming into Greek COL/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'rectum'?
explanation: Rect/o derives from Latin 'rectum' (straight intestine). Found in rectoscopy, rectocele, rectal, rectosigmoid, and proctorectoscopy.
statement: The combining form for rectum is rect/o.
wowFactor: The rectum was named 'rectum' (Latin for 'straight') by Galen, who thought it was straight in humans — it actually has curves!

variants.question: Rect/o is the combining form for which structure?
Colorectal cancer affects the colon and ___.

visualDescription: English RECTUM transforming into Latin RECT/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'appendix'?
explanation: Appendic/o derives from Latin 'appendix' (appendage). Found in appendicitis, appendectomy, appendicostomy, and appendiceal.
statement: The combining form for appendix is appendic/o.
wowFactor: Appendectomy (appendic/o + ectomy) is one of the most common emergency surgeries performed worldwide.

variants.question: Appendic/o is the combining form for which structure?
Appendicitis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English APPENDIX transforming into Latin APPENDIC/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'gallbladder'?
explanation: Cholecyst/o derives from Greek 'chole' (bile) + 'kystis' (bladder/sac). Found in cholecystitis, cholecystectomy, cholecystography, and cholecystokinin.
statement: The combining form for gallbladder is cholecyst/o.
wowFactor: Cholecystectomy (gallbladder removal) is one of the most common surgeries — it literally means 'cutting out the bile bladder'.

variants.question: Cholecyst/o is the combining form for which organ?
Cholecystitis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English GALLBLADDER transforming into Greek CHOLECYST/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'pancreas'?
explanation: Pancreat/o derives from Greek 'pankreas' (all flesh). Found in pancreatology, pancreatitis, pancreatectomy, and pancreaticoduodenectomy.
statement: The combining form for pancreas is pancreat/o.
wowFactor: The pancreas is named 'all flesh' in Greek (pan = all, kreas = flesh) because early anatomists thought it was just a solid mass of tissue.

variants.question: Pancreat/o is the combining form for which organ?
Pancreatitis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English PANCREAS transforming into Greek PANCREAT/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'spleen'?
explanation: Splen/o derives from Greek 'splen' (spleen). Found in splenology, splenitis, splenomegaly, splenectomy, and hepatosplenomegaly.
statement: The combining form for spleen is splen/o.
wowFactor: Splenomegaly (enlarged spleen) is a key sign in many infections — splen/o (spleen) + mega (large) + ly (condition of).

variants.question: Splen/o is the combining form for which organ?
Splenomegaly refers to an abnormally enlarged ___.

visualDescription: English SPLEEN transforming into Greek SPLEN/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'bladder'?
explanation: Cyst/o derives from Greek 'kystis' (bladder, sac). Found in cystology, cystitis, cystoscopy, cystectomy, and cholecystitis.
statement: The combining form for bladder is cyst/o.
wowFactor: Cyst/o means both bladder AND any fluid-filled sac — that is why a cyst on your skin uses the same root as cystitis (bladder infection).

variants.question: Cyst/o is the combining form for which organ?
Cystitis is inflammation of the urinary ___.

visualDescription: English BLADDER transforming into Greek CYST/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'urethra'?
explanation: Urethr/o derives from Greek 'ourethra' (tube that carries urine out of body). Found in urethroscopy, urethritis, urethroplasty, and urethrocele.
statement: The combining form for urethra is urethr/o.
wowFactor: Urethritis (inflammation of the urethra) is a key symptom in several sexually transmitted infections — early recognition matters.

variants.question: Urethr/o is the combining form for which structure?
Which combining form refers to the urethra (NOT the ureter)?

visualDescription: English URETHRA transforming into Greek URETHR/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'ureter'?
explanation: Ureter/o derives from Greek 'oureter' (ureter, tube from kidney to bladder). Found in ureteroscopy, ureteritis, ureteroplasty, and ureterocele.
statement: The combining form for ureter is ureter/o.
wowFactor: Ureter/o and urethr/o differ by just two letters but refer to completely different tubes — ureteral stents keep blocked ureters open.

variants.question: Ureter/o is the combining form for which structure?
Which combining form refers to the tube connecting kidney to bladder?

visualDescription: English URETER transforming into Greek URETER/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'uterus'?
explanation: Hyster/o derives from Greek 'hystera' (uterus). Found in hysterectomy, hysteroscopy, hysteria (historically linked to the uterus), and hysterosalpingography.
statement: The combining form for uterus is hyster/o.
wowFactor: The word 'hysteria' was once blamed on the uterus — ancient Greeks thought wandering wombs caused emotional disturbances. Completely wrong, but etymologically fascinating!

variants.question: Hyster/o is the combining form for which organ?
A hysterectomy is surgical removal of the ___.

visualDescription: English UTERUS transforming into Greek HYSTER/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'ovary'?
explanation: Oophor/o derives from Greek 'oophoros' (egg carrier). Found in oophoritis, oophorectomy, oophorocystectomy, and salpingo-oophorectomy.
statement: The combining form for ovary is oophor/o.
wowFactor: Oophor/o literally means 'egg carrier' in Greek — oo (egg) + phor (carry) — perfectly describing the ovary's function of holding and releasing eggs.

variants.question: Oophor/o is the combining form for which organ?
Oophoritis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English OVARY transforming into Greek OOPHOR/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'breast'?
explanation: Mast/o derives from Greek 'mastos' (breast). Found in mastology, mastitis, mastectomy, mastodynia, and gynecomastia.
statement: The combining form for breast is mast/o.
wowFactor: A mastectomy (breast removal) uses mast/o (breast) + ectomy (surgical removal) — one of the most common cancer surgeries for women.

variants.question: Mast/o is the combining form for which structure?
Mastitis is inflammation of the ___ tissue.

visualDescription: English BREAST transforming into Greek MAST/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'testis'?
explanation: Orchi/o derives from Greek 'orchis' (testicle). Found in orchitis, orchidectomy, orchiopexy, cryptorchidism, and orchiepididymitis.
statement: The combining form for testis is orchi/o.
wowFactor: The orchid flower was named after the Greek word for testicle because its twin bulbous roots resembled testes — botanical nomenclature can be surprising!

variants.question: Orchi/o is the combining form for which organ?
Orchitis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English TESTIS transforming into Greek ORCHI/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'prostate'?
explanation: Prostat/o derives from Greek 'prostates' (one who stands before). Found in prostatology, prostatitis, prostatectomy, and prostate-specific antigen (PSA).
statement: The combining form for prostate is prostat/o.
wowFactor: The prostate was named 'one who stands before' in Greek because it sits in front of (before) the bladder — an anatomically accurate name.

variants.question: Prostat/o is the combining form for which gland?
Prostatitis is inflammation of the ___ gland.

visualDescription: English PROSTATE transforming into Greek PROSTAT/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'thyroid'?
explanation: Thyr/o derives from Greek 'thyreos' (shield-shaped). Found in thyrology, thyroiditis, thyroidectomy, thyroxine (T4), and hypothyroidism.
statement: The combining form for thyroid is thyr/o.
wowFactor: The thyroid gland is named for its shield shape (Greek thyreos = large oblong shield) — its butterfly shape wraps around the trachea like armor.

variants.question: Thyr/o is the combining form for which gland?
Thyroiditis is inflammation of the ___ gland.

visualDescription: English THYROID transforming into Greek THYR/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'adrenal'?
explanation: Adren/o derives from Latin 'ad' (near) + 'ren' (kidney). Found in adrenology, adrenalitis, adrenalectomy, adrenaline, and adrenocorticotropic hormone (ACTH).
statement: The combining form for adrenal is adren/o.
wowFactor: Adrenaline (epinephrine) gets its name from adren/o — the adrenal glands release this hormone during the fight-or-flight response.

variants.question: Adren/o is the combining form for which gland?
Adrenaline (epinephrine) is secreted by the ___ gland.

visualDescription: English ADRENAL transforming into Latin ADREN/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'gland'?
explanation: Aden/o derives from Greek 'aden' (gland). Found in adenology, adenitis, adenoma, adenocarcinoma, adenopathy, and adenoids.
statement: The combining form for gland is aden/o.
wowFactor: Adenocarcinoma is the most common type of cancer in many organs (lung, colon, breast) — aden/o (gland) + carcin (cancer) + oma (tumor).

variants.question: Aden/o is the combining form for which structure?
Adenitis is inflammation of a ___.

visualDescription: English GLAND transforming into Greek ADEN/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'blood vessel'?
explanation: Angi/o derives from Greek 'angeion' (vessel, container). Found in angiology, angiogram, angioplasty, angiography, and angiogenesis.
statement: The combining form for blood vessel is angi/o.
wowFactor: Angioplasty (balloon procedure to open blocked arteries) literally means 'vessel molding' — angi/o (vessel) + plasty (surgical reshaping).

variants.question: Angi/o is the combining form for which structure?
An angiogram is an X-ray image of a ___ or vessels.

visualDescription: English BLOOD VESSEL transforming into Greek ANGI/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'artery'?
explanation: Arteri/o derives from Greek 'arteria' (windpipe, artery). Found in arteriology, arteriosclerosis, arteriogram, arteritis, and endarterectomy.
statement: The combining form for artery is arteri/o.
wowFactor: The ancient Greeks called arteries 'windpipes' because they found them empty after death — they thought arteries carried air, not blood!

variants.question: Arteri/o is the combining form for which vessel type?
Arteriosclerosis is hardening of the ___.

visualDescription: English ARTERY transforming into Greek ARTERI/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'vein'?
explanation: Phleb/o derives from Greek 'phleps/phlebos' (vein). Found in phlebology, phlebitis, phlebotomy, phlebectomy, and thrombophlebitis.
statement: The combining form for vein is phleb/o.
wowFactor: Phlebotomy (drawing blood from a vein) uses phleb/o (vein) + tomy (cutting) — phlebotomists perform this every day in clinics and hospitals.

variants.question: Phleb/o is the combining form for which vessel type?
Phlebotomy literally means 'cutting' which type of vessel?

visualDescription: English VEIN transforming into Greek PHLEB/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'blood'?
explanation: Hemat/o derives from Greek 'haima/haimatos' (blood). Found in hematology, hematoma, hematuria, hemoglobin, hematopoiesis, and hemophilia.
statement: The combining form for blood is hemat/o.
wowFactor: Hemoglobin (the protein that carries oxygen in red blood cells) uses hem/o (blood) + globin (protein) — it is what makes blood red.

variants.question: Hemat/o is the combining form for which substance?
Hematuria (blood in the ___) is a sign of urinary tract disease.

visualDescription: English BLOOD transforming into Greek HEMAT/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'cartilage'?
explanation: Chondr/o derives from Greek 'chondros' (cartilage, grain). Found in chondrology, chondritis, chondromalacia, chondroplasty, and osteochondritis.
statement: The combining form for cartilage is chondr/o.
wowFactor: Chondromalacia patellae (runner's knee) literally means 'soft cartilage of the kneecap' — chondr/o (cartilage) + malacia (softening).

variants.question: Chondr/o is the combining form for which tissue?
Chondritis is inflammation of ___ tissue.

visualDescription: English CARTILAGE transforming into Greek CHONDR/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'tendon'?
explanation: Tend/o derives from Latin 'tendere' (to stretch, extend). Found in tendonitis (tendinitis), tendoplasty, tenorrhaphy, tenosynovitis, and tenodesis.
statement: The combining form for tendon is tend/o.
wowFactor: Tendinitis is inflammation of a tendon — the Achilles tendon is the strongest tendon in the body yet one of the most commonly injured.

variants.question: Tend/o is the combining form for which tissue?
Tendinitis is inflammation of a ___.

visualDescription: English TENDON transforming into Latin TEND/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'skull'?
explanation: Crani/o derives from Greek 'kranion' (skull). Found in craniology, craniotomy, cranial nerves, cranioplasty, and intracranial pressure.
statement: The combining form for skull is crani/o.
wowFactor: There are 12 cranial nerves — all named 'cranial' because they emerge directly from the crani/o (skull/brain), not the spinal cord.

variants.question: Crani/o is the combining form for which structure?
A craniotomy is a surgical opening of the ___.

visualDescription: English SKULL transforming into Greek CRANI/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'vertebra'?
explanation: Vertebr/o derives from Latin 'vertebra' (joint, vertebra). Found in vertebrology, vertebral, vertebroplasty, spondylitis, and spondylosis.
statement: The combining form for vertebra is vertebr/o.
wowFactor: Spondylitis (spinal inflammation) uses the Greek synonym spondyl/o — both vertebr/o (Latin) and spondyl/o (Greek) refer to the same structure.

variants.question: Vertebr/o is the combining form for which structure?
Vertebroplasty is a procedure to repair a fractured ___.

visualDescription: English VERTEBRA transforming into Latin VERTEBR/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'spinal cord'?
explanation: Myel/o derives from Greek 'myelos' (marrow, spinal cord). Found in myelology, myelitis, myelopathy, osteomyelitis (bone marrow), and myelogram.
statement: The combining form for spinal cord is myel/o.
wowFactor: Myel/o has a dual meaning — it means both 'spinal cord' AND 'bone marrow'. Context determines which is meant: myelitis vs. myeloma (bone marrow cancer).

variants.question: Myel/o is the combining form for which structure?
Myelitis is inflammation of the ___.

visualDescription: English SPINAL CORD transforming into Greek MYEL/O with arrow

quizQuestion: What is the combining form for 'retina'?
explanation: Retin/o derives from Latin 'rete' (net) — the retina has a net-like structure of photoreceptors. Found in retinology, retinitis, retinopathy, retinoblastoma, and retinoscopy.
statement: The combining form for retina is retin/o.
wowFactor: Diabetic retinopathy (retin/o + pathy = disease of the retina) is the leading cause of blindness in working-age adults worldwide.

variants.question: Retin/o is the combining form for which eye structure?
Diabetic retinopathy is a disease of the ___ caused by diabetes.

visualDescription: English RETINA transforming into Latin RETIN/O with arrow
